description: Top 5 cities by total revenue
question: What are the top 5 cities by total revenue?
sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with users, group by city, SUM grand_total, ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

description: All cities ranked by revenue (10 cities)
question: Show me revenue breakdown by city across all cities.
sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with users, group by city, SUM grand_total, ORDER BY DESC

description: Bottom 3 cities by revenue
question: Which cities have the lowest total revenue?
sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue ASC LIMIT 3

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with users, group by city, SUM grand_total, ORDER BY ASC LIMIT 3
completed_steps.sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue ASC LIMIT 3

description: Top 5 products by revenue
question: What are the top 5 products by total revenue?
sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products, group by product name, SUM total_price, ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

description: Top 5 products by units sold
question: Which products have sold the most units overall?
sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_units_sold DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products, group by product name, SUM quantity, ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_units_sold DESC LIMIT 5

description: Top 5 products by number of orders
question: Which products appear in the most orders?
sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS order_count FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products, group by product name, COUNT DISTINCT order_id, ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS order_count FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 5

description: Top 5 products by revenue with category
question: What are the top 5 revenue-generating products and which categories do they belong to?
sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, c.name AS category, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY p.name, c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories, group by product and category name, SUM total_price, ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT p.name AS product_name, c.name AS category, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY p.name, c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

description: Full order status breakdown by count
question: What is the distribution of orders by status?
sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC

completed_steps.objective: Group orders by status, COUNT order_id, ORDER BY count DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC

description: Order status with percentage breakdown
question: What percentage of orders are in each status?
sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count, ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC

completed_steps.objective: Group orders by status, COUNT and compute percentage using window function, ORDER BY count DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count, ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC

description: All categories by total revenue (16 categories)
question: How does revenue break down across product categories?
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories, group by category name, SUM total_price, ORDER BY DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(SUM(oi.total_price)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

description: Categories by order count
question: Which product categories have the most orders?
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories, group by category, COUNT DISTINCT order_id, ORDER BY DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT c.name AS category, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC

description: Categories by average order value
question: Which product categories have the highest average order value?
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(AVG(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories, group by category, AVG grand_total, ORDER BY DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT c.name AS category, ROUND(AVG(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC

description: Overall KPIs: AOV, total orders, unique customers
question: What are our key customer metrics — average order value, total orders, and unique customer count?
sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(*) AS total_orders, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_customers FROM orders

completed_steps.objective: Aggregate orders: AVG grand_total, COUNT orders, COUNT DISTINCT user_id
completed_steps.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(*) AS total_orders, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_customers FROM orders

description: Repeat buyers count
question: How many customers have placed more than one order?
sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS repeat_buyers FROM (SELECT user_id FROM orders GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) sub

completed_steps.objective: Count customers with more than 1 order using subquery with HAVING COUNT > 1
completed_steps.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS repeat_buyers FROM (SELECT user_id FROM orders GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) sub

description: Top 5 customers by total spend
question: Who are our top 5 customers by total spend?
sql: SELECT u.full_name, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id, u.full_name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with users, group by customer, SUM grand_total, COUNT orders, ORDER BY spend DESC LIMIT 5
completed_steps.sql: SELECT u.full_name, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.user_id, u.full_name ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5

description: Monthly revenue trend over 12 months
question: Show me the monthly revenue trend for the past year.
sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(SUM(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

completed_steps.objective: Group orders by month using DATE_TRUNC, SUM grand_total, ORDER BY month ASC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(SUM(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

description: Monthly order count trend over 12 months
question: How has the number of orders changed month over month?
sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

completed_steps.objective: Group orders by month using DATE_TRUNC, COUNT orders, ORDER BY month ASC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

description: Monthly average order value trend over 12 months
question: How has the average order value changed month over month?
sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(AVG(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

completed_steps.objective: Group orders by month using DATE_TRUNC, AVG grand_total, ORDER BY month ASC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(AVG(grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

description: Revenue and transaction count by payment method
question: How does revenue break down by payment method?
sql: SELECT method AS payment_method, ROUND(SUM(amount)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue, COUNT(*) AS transaction_count FROM payments GROUP BY method ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

completed_steps.objective: Group payments by method, SUM amount and COUNT transactions, ORDER BY revenue DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT method AS payment_method, ROUND(SUM(amount)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue, COUNT(*) AS transaction_count FROM payments GROUP BY method ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

description: Order count by payment method
question: Which payment method is used most frequently for placing orders?
sql: SELECT p.method AS payment_method, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id GROUP BY p.method ORDER BY order_count DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with payments, group by payment method, COUNT DISTINCT orders, ORDER BY DESC
completed_steps.sql: SELECT p.method AS payment_method, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id GROUP BY p.method ORDER BY order_count DESC

description: Empty result — no orders exist before 2025
question: What was the revenue by city in 2024?
sql: SELECT u.city, ROUND(SUM(o.grand_total)::numeric, 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id WHERE o.created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND o.created_at < '2025-01-01' GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

completed_steps.objective: Join orders with users, filter created_at in 2024, group by city, SUM grand_total
